description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rounded top and a slightly flattened base, resting on a patch of bare, compacted earth. The rock's surface is predominantly light beige with patches of white and subtle brownish streaks, suggesting weathering and exposure to the elements. Its texture appears rough and granular, with visible pitting and natural indentations across its surface. The base of the rock is partially embedded in the ground, with a few small pebbles and debris scattered nearby. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side more sloped and the other more vertical, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. This type of rock is commonly found in landscaped urban parks or as a decorative barrier along sidewalks and pathways.
description: A rough, light beige rock with a rounded top and irregular, weathered surface, partially embedded in compacted earth. Its asymmetrical shape and natural texture make it suitable as a decorative or barrier element in urban settings.
description_view_0: The rock appears upright with its broadest, most vertical face visible, showing a mix of beige and white coloration and a slightly pointed top.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the elongated side of the rock, revealing more of its sloped surface and brownish streaks along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view shows a more rounded, less vertical face, with the rock's base more visible and some small debris nearby.
description_view_3: From this side, the rock's asymmetry is clear, with a flatter, wider surface and subtle color variations across its rough exterior.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or sidewalk edge
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is no visible evidence that the rock is intentionally incorporated into urban infrastructure.
